what_it_does: Defines the npm package manifest for a private ESM React application named company-canvas, listing runtime dependencies (react, react-dom, tldraw), dev dependencies (Vite, TypeScript, @vitejs/plugin-react), and two Vite-based scripts for development and production builds.
observations: Package is private and uses ESM via "type": "module".
Scripts are limited to "dev" (vite --host 127.0.0.1 --port 5173) and "build" (vite build).
Runtime dependencies include react ^18.3.1, react-dom ^18.3.1, and tldraw ^3.13.1.
Dev dependencies include @vitejs/plugin-react ^4.3.4, typescript ^5.6.3, and vite ^6.0.7.
No version, description, license, test, lint, or typecheck scripts are present.
standouts: tldraw is the dominant domain signal, indicating a whiteboard/drawing canvas UI.
The dev server is explicitly bound to 127.0.0.1:5173 rather than using Vite defaults.
It is a minimal, modern Vite+React package with no extra tooling scripts.
conventions: Uses caret semver ranges for dependencies.
concerns: Only dev and build scripts exist; no test, lint, or typecheck scripts.
Dev server is restricted to loopback, which may limit LAN/mobile testing.
tldraw is pinned with a caret on a major version, so upgrades could introduce breaking changes.
notes: Vite React app package manifest for a tldraw canvas; scripts are limited to dev and build.
questions: What specific tldraw features or custom shapes does the app implement?
Why is the dev server explicitly bound to 127.0.0.1 instead of the default host?
Is there a separate tsconfig.json or any TypeScript configuration file?
Are there plans for test, lint, or preview scripts?
purpose_vs_actual: n/a — no explicit purpose is stated beyond package metadata; the name and tldraw dependency imply a canvas UI, and the scripts provide only dev/build tooling.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to set up a private, ESM React application with an interactive whiteboard/drawing surface via tldraw, bundled and served by Vite.
novelty: The dev script explicitly binds Vite to 127.0.0.1:5173; otherwise it is a conventional Vite+React+tldraw package.json.
contribution: It contributes the package identity, ESM configuration, dependency contract (React, tldraw, Vite toolchain), and the dev/build script interface for the canvas app.
summary_for_embedding: This package.json declares a private ESM React application named company-canvas that depends on tldraw and uses Vite for development and production builds.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to configure the npm package and build toolchain for a Vite-based React canvas app integrating tldraw.
